title: Indigofera depressa
taxonomy: Indigofera depressa is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification places it in the subfamily Faboideae. Indigofera depressa is further classified within the genus Indigofera, which consists of approximately 700 species of plants. The genus Indigofera is often associated with the production of indigo dye. Indigofera depressa is native to tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Indigofera depressa, also known as Small-leaved Indigo, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, blue-violet flowers and narrow, flat leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Indigofera depressa include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as indole alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Indigofera depressa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
